10.argument: Let d = d(w) < 10. Since k > p and w < q < 2p by Bertrand, it is enough to show (10 - 2) ln p > (d - 2) ln(2p), equivalently p^(10 - d) > 2^(d - 2). The largest surviving threshold comes from d = 9, namely p > 2^7 = 128. The committed exact no-early-spoiler base is contiguous through p < 5e9, so the threshold prime 131 closes the finite remainder.

14.argument: Let d = d(w) < 14. Again Bertrand reduces the comparison to p^(14 - d) > 2^(d - 2). The odd branch d = 13 is automatic because min_n_with_tau(13) = 4096, so any d = 13 winner satisfies w >= 4096 and w < 2p, hence p > 2048. Among the remaining classes the largest threshold is d = 12, giving p > 2^5 = 32. The threshold prime 37 lies inside the committed exact base through p < 5e9, so class 14 closes.

18.argument: Let d = d(w) < 18. Bertrand gives the sufficient condition p^(18 - d) > 2^(d - 2). The odd branch d = 17 is automatic because min_n_with_tau(17) = 65536, so w >= 65536 and w < 2p force p > 32768. The largest remaining nonautomatic threshold is d = 16, which again gives p > 2^7 = 128. The threshold prime 131 lies inside the committed exact base through p < 5e9, so class 18 closes.

notes: Loaded the committed 2e7 admissibility frontier, the committed 1e9 retained admissibility frontier, the committed 1e9 and 5e9 exact no-early-spoiler aggregates, and the admissibility extractor. Re-verified the witness function min_n_with_tau(d) from gwr/experiments/proof/large_prime_reducer.py before accepting any closure. This run closes 13 of the 18 requested residual classes: {10,14,18,20,22,26,27,28,30,42,44,54,60}. The remaining open classes are exactly {36,40,50,52,56}, and each reduces to one unresolved odd winner class {35,39,49,51,55} together with a finite p-window above the exact 5e9 base. A fresh local extraction over output/gwr_proof/scan_checkpoints_5e9 also surfaced unsupported class 34 in the retained checkpoint summary; that does not change the per-class verdicts requested here, but it means the 1e9 residual table is not stable under extension.
